name: Biloxi Teachers
description_short: Federal credit union established in 1965 serving educators and educational organizations in Biloxi, Mississippi with savings accounts, auto loans, personal loans, and CDs.
description_long:
Biloxi Teachers Federal Credit Union was established in 1965 and is headquartered in Biloxi, Mississippi. As a mission-driven credit union, it specifically serves educators and employees of educational organizations, reflecting its roots in the teaching profession. The institution operates as a not-for-profit cooperative, meaning profits are returned to members through better rates and lower fees rather than distributed to shareholders.

The credit union offers a comprehensive range of personal financial services including savings accounts, share draft (checking) accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), auto loans for new and used vehicles, personal loans, mortgages, IRAs/KEOGH accounts, wire transfers, and financial counseling. Members can access services through online banking, a mobile app available on iOS and Android, bill pay functionality, and in-person at their single branch location in Biloxi. Current sample rates include 3.32% APR for new vehicle loans, 4.57% for used vehicle loans, and 10.39% for unsecured personal loans.

What distinguishes Biloxi Teachers is its member-focused cooperative structure and field-of-membership orientation toward education professionals. With over 1,300 members and approximately $10.1 million in assets, it maintains a community-oriented approach with over 3 dedicated employees. The institution provides financial calculators, comparative interest rate tools, and a community Q&A forum to support member financial literacy.

However, potential members should note that this is a very small credit union with limited infrastructure: only 1 physical branch and 0 ATMs, which may limit accessibility for members outside the immediate Biloxi area. The membership is restricted to those working for eligible educational organizations, so not all consumers can join. As a small institution, product offerings and competitive rates may be more limited compared to larger regional or national credit unions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Not-for-profit structure means better rates and lower fees compared to for-profit banks
Established 60+ year history (founded 1965) demonstrates stability and longevity
Full-service offerings including mortgages, auto loans, personal loans, CDs, and savings accounts
Mobile app available on both iOS and Android for convenient banking
Online bill pay and external account transfers available to members
Membership eligibility focused on education sector provides community connection
Financial counseling services available to help members with planning
cons: Extremely limited branch network with only 1 physical location in Biloxi, Mississippi and zero ATMs
Membership restricted to employees of educational organizations—general consumers cannot join
Very small asset base ($10.1 million) and member base (1,300 members) limits resources and product competitiveness
No reviews available on the profile, providing no consumer feedback on service quality
Lobby currently closed at time of profile data, indicating potential operational limitations
best_for: Teachers and school administrators in the Biloxi, Mississippi area
Employees of educational institutions seeking community-focused financial services
Educators wanting to support a member-owned, not-for-profit institution
Members of eligible educational organizations seeking personalized financial counseling
diagnosis: Best for educators and educational organization employees in Biloxi who value community-oriented, not-for-profit banking and can access the single physical branch. Primary limitation is geographic and membership restriction—non-education professionals cannot join, and the single branch location with no ATMs makes this impractical for members outside the immediate Biloxi area.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
